# services.bind

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.bind`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.bind.blockedNetworks` | `list of string` | What networks are just blocked. |
| `services.bind.cacheNetworks` | `list of string` | What networks are allowed to use us as a resolver. Note that this is for recursive queries -- all networks are allowed to query zones configured with the `zones` option by default (although this may be overridden within each zone's configuration, via the `allowQuery` option). It is recommended that you limit cacheNetworks to avoid your server being used for DNS amplification attacks. |
| `services.bind.checkConfig` | `boolean` | Check configuration. The configuration will not be checked if you override the config file with `configFile`. |
| `services.bind.configFile` | `absolute path` | Overridable config file to use for named. By default, that generated by nixos. If overriden, it will not be checked by named-checkconf. |
| `services.bind.directory` | `string` | Working directory of BIND. |
| `services.bind.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable BIND domain name server. |
| `services.bind.extraArgs` | `list of string` | Additional command-line arguments to pass to named. |
| `services.bind.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra lines to be added verbatim to the generated named configuration file. |
| `services.bind.extraOptions` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra lines to be added verbatim to the options section of the generated named configuration file. |
| `services.bind.forward` | `one of "first", "only"` | Whether to forward 'first' (try forwarding but lookup directly if forwarding fails) or 'only'. |
| `services.bind.forwarders` | `list of string` | List of servers we should forward requests to. |
| `services.bind.ipv4Only` | `boolean` | Only use ipv4, even if the host supports ipv6. |
| `services.bind.listenOn` | `list of string` | Interfaces to listen on. |
| `services.bind.listenOnIpv6` | `list of string` | Ipv6 interfaces to listen on. |
| `services.bind.listenOnIpv6Port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Ipv6 port to listen on. |
| `services.bind.listenOnPort` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Port to listen on. |
| `services.bind.package` | `package` | The bind package to use. |
| `services.bind.zones` | `(attribute set of (submodule)) or (list of (attribute set)) convertible to it` | List of zones we claim authority over. |
| `services.bind.zones.<name>.allowQuery` | `list of string` | List of address ranges allowed to query this zone. Instead of the address(es), this may instead contain the single string "any". |
| `services.bind.zones.<name>.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra zone config to be appended at the end of the zone section. |
| `services.bind.zones.<name>.file` | `string or absolute path` | Zone file resource records contain columns of data, separated by whitespace, that define the record. |
| `services.bind.zones.<name>.master` | `boolean` | Master=false means slave server |
| `services.bind.zones.<name>.masters` | `list of string` | List of servers for inclusion in stub and secondary zones. |
| `services.bind.zones.<name>.name` | `string` | Name of the zone. |
| `services.bind.zones.<name>.slaves` | `list of string` | Addresses who may request zone transfers. |
